rulings.Case Title: TONY FU VS. DEMAS YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Kaman Liu'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Of Plaintiff'S Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 12, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT KAMAN LIU'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR MANDATORY DISMISSAL OF PLAINTIFF'S ACTION is OFF CALENDAR. No valid proof of service on file. Proof of service filed on August 12, 2022 does not comply with CCP 1013b. This is the second time this motion is filed and the second time the motion is not procedurally code-compliant. The Court further notes that on the merits the motion again omits discussion of relevant facts that are on the record in this action, e.g. legal significance, if any, of this action being listed as an asset of the estate in the bankruptcy petition; August 30, 2017 Bankruptcy Court order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TONY FU VS. DEMAS YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Defendant Kaman Lius Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 12, 2022 line 1. CROSS-DEFENDANT STELL CHEN'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DISMISS DEFENDANT KAMAN LIU'S CROSS-COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Cross-Complaint provides no authority for the proposition that a cross-complaint filed by Liu against Chen is subject to automatic bankruptcy stay due to Fu's filing for bankruptcy. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: PHUONG T. NGUYEN VS. LONDON BREED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Phuong T. Nguyen'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider Its Ruling On Order Filed From May 27, 2022 Concerning Plaintiff'S Demurrer To Answer And Motion To Strike Answer; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Supporting Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 12, 2022 line 3. PLAINTIFF PHUONG T. NGUYEN'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO RECONSIDER IT'S RULING ON ORDER FILED MAY 27, 2022 CONCERNING PLAINTIFF'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER AND MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER is Denied. Moving party failed to carry its burden under CCP 1008. In ruling on the demurrer and the motion to strike the Court considered all legal authority cited, the pleadings challenged, its own records and all filings therein. To the extent, Plaintiff seeks a ruling on the request for judicial notice of the same, the request for judicial notice was granted and the documents considered as stated above. Untimely and improper request for a statement of decision (CRC 3.1590(a) and (n)) does not give rise to a motion under CCP 1008. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
